name: John Dee
sig: English mathematician, astronomer, astrologer, and occult philosopher; advisor to Elizabeth I; developed Enochian angel magic system; author of Monas Hieroglyphica

name: Giovanni Pico della Mirandola
sig: Italian philosopher; author of Oration on the Dignity of Man and 900 syncretic Theses; pioneer of Christian Kabbalah; attempted synthesis of all philosophical traditions

name: Giordano Bruno
sig: Italian Dominican friar, philosopher, cosmologist; proponent of infinite universe and Hermetic magic; developed Lullian memory arts; burned at the stake for heresy

name: Heinrich Cornelius Agrippa
sig: German polymath and occult philosopher; author of De Occulta Philosophia, the foundational Renaissance synthesis of natural, celestial, and ceremonial magic

name: Marsilio Ficino
sig: Italian philosopher and Catholic priest; head of Florentine Platonic Academy; translated Corpus Hermeticum and Plato into Latin; author of Theologia Platonica and De Vita

name: Johannes Trithemius
sig: German Benedictine abbot; author of Steganographia (cryptography/angel magic) and Polygraphia; teacher of Agrippa

name: Johannes Reuchlin
sig: German humanist and Hebraist; author of De Verbo Mirifico and De Arte Cabalistica; defender of Jewish books; pioneer of Christian Kabbalah alongside Pico

name: Robert Fludd
sig: English Paracelsian physician and Rosicrucian philosopher; developed elaborate cosmological diagrams integrating Hermetic, Kabbalistic, and alchemical symbolism

name: Athanasius Kircher
sig: German Jesuit polymath; Egyptologist and universalist scholar; attempted decipherment of hieroglyphics; studied magnetism, acoustics, and geo-cosmology

name: Jacob Boehme
sig: German Christian mystic and theologian; author of Aurora; developed theosophical system drawing on alchemy and Kabbalistic imagery; influenced later German Idealism

name: Francis Mercury van Helmont
sig: Flemish philosopher and Kabbalist; advocate of transmigration of souls; bridge figure between Renaissance Kabbalah and Enlightenment thought

name: Ramon Llull
sig: Catalan philosopher and mystic; inventor of the Ars Magna combinatory system; influenced Bruno's memory arts and later computational thinking

name: Paracelsus
sig: Swiss physician, alchemist, and astrologer; founder of iatrochemistry; reformed medical practice using chemical remedies; developed tria prima (salt, sulphur, mercury) theory

name: Hermes Trismegistus
sig: Legendary author of the Corpus Hermeticum; syncretic figure combining Greek Hermes and Egyptian Thoth; central to prisca theologia tradition; real texts date to 2nd-3rd century CE

name: Plato
sig: Athenian philosopher; Timaeus provided cosmological framework for Renaissance Neoplatonic magic; Republic's demiurge concept central to Hermetic thought

name: Plotinus
sig: Neoplatonic philosopher; Enneads provided the metaphysical framework (One, Intellect, Soul, Matter) that structured all Renaissance magical philosophy

name: Proclus
sig: Late Neoplatonic philosopher; systematized theurgy; influenced Ficino and the Renaissance understanding of divine names and ritual practice

name: Iamblichus
sig: Neoplatonic philosopher; De Mysteriis provided philosophical justification for theurgy; crucial link between philosophy and ritual magic

name: Al-Kindi
sig: Arab philosopher; De Radiis Stellarum theorized celestial influence and ray theory; key intermediary transmitting Greek magical philosophy to Latin West

name: Nicholas of Cusa
sig: German cardinal and philosopher; De Docta Ignorantia and coincidentia oppositorum influenced Pico and Bruno; bridge between medieval and Renaissance thought

name: Albertus Magnus
sig: German Dominican friar and philosopher; Speculum Astronomiae distinguished licit from illicit astrology; key medieval authority on natural magic

name: Roger Bacon
sig: English Franciscan friar; advocated scientia experimentalis; Opus Majus included sections on optics and natural magic; forerunner of empirical approach

name: Frances A. Yates
sig: British historian; Giordano Bruno and the Hermetic Tradition (1964) established the 'Yates thesis' linking Hermeticism to the Scientific Revolution

name: Brian P. Copenhaver
sig: American historian of philosophy; leading authority on Renaissance Hermetica and Ficino; editor and translator of key primary sources

name: D.P. Walker
sig: British historian; Spiritual and Demonic Magic from Ficino to Campanella (1958) mapped the intellectual history of Renaissance magical philosophy

name: Paola Zambelli
sig: Italian historian of science and magic; major works on the ambiguous nature of Renaissance magic, astrology, and the white/black magic distinction

name: Charles Zika
sig: Australian historian; specialist in witchcraft, visual culture, and early modern European demonology

name: Vittoria Perrone Compagni
sig: Italian historian of medieval and Renaissance philosophy; editor of critical editions of Agrippa and other magical texts

name: Liana Saif
sig: Historian of Arabic occult sciences; author on Arabic influences on Western occult philosophy; key source for Islamic intellectual genealogy of Renaissance magic
